question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: setBloom(byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction whose bloom field is crafted to be exactly 256 bytes of non-zero data repeated at high frequency, causing Arrays.equals() comparisons against EMPTY_BLOOM to consume disproportionate CPU across all importer nodes processing that partition, degrading 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: setBloom(byte[])] Can an unprivileged user submit thousands of contract calls each with a bloom payload of exactly 256 bytes that is not all-zero, forcing every importer node to allocate and retain a full 256-byte array per ContractResult in heap, causing coordinated OOM across 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: toContractTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged user submit a ContractResult where payerAccountId is null, causing a NullPointerException at payerAccountId.getId() inside toContractTransactionHash(), which if unhandled propagates to crash the importer processing thread on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: toContractTransactionHash()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction that results in a null transactionHash being passed to ContractTransactionHash.builder().hash(transactionHash), and if downstream hash-based indexing dereferences this null, cause a crash in the processing pipeline on 30%+ of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: isNew()] Can an unprivileged user submit duplicate contract transactions with the same consensusTimestamp (natural ID), and because isNew() always returns true, cause Hibernate to attempt duplicate inserts that throw database constraint violations, crashing the batch-insert pipeline on 30%+ of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getId()] Can an unprivileged user submit a ContractResult with a null consensusTimestamp, causing getId() to return null and triggering a NullPointerException in Hibernate's identity resolution, which crashes the entity persistence thread on 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: functionParameters] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract call with an extremely large functionParameters byte array (e.g., near the protobuf message size limit), causing each importer node to allocate a large heap object per transaction, and when many such transactions are processed concurrently, triggering OOM on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: functionResult] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract transaction whose functionResult byte array is maximally sized, and because this field is described as temporary and retained in memory alongside other fields, cause heap exhaustion on 30%+ of importer nodes when many such records are buffered before flush?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: failedInitcode] Can an unprivileged user submit a failed contract creation with a maximally large failedInitcode byte array, causing each importer node to retain the full initcode in memory as part of the ContractResult object, leading to heap exhaustion on 30%+ of nodes when processing a burst of such transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: callResult] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract call that returns a maximally large callResult byte array, and because ContractResult holds this in memory until persistence, cause coordinated heap pressure on 30%+ of importer nodes processing the same record stream partition?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: createdContractIds] Can an unprivileged user deploy a contract that creates an extremely large number of child contracts, resulting in a createdContractIds List<Long> with thousands of entries per ContractResult, causing memory exhaustion on 30%+ of importer nodes when many such records are processed simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: errorMessage] Can an unprivileged user trigger a contract execution that produces an extremely long errorMessage string, and because this String is stored without length validation in ContractResult, cause excessive memory allocation on 30%+ of importer nodes when processing a flood of such failed transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: gasLimit] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract transaction with gasLimit set to Long.MAX_VALUE, and if downstream gas calculation logic (e.g., in SidecarProcessingResult.calculateGasConsumed) performs arithmetic on gasLimit without overflow checks, cause an arithmetic exception that crashes the processing thread on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: gasUsed] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract transaction where gasUsed exceeds gasLimit (an inconsistent state), and if importer nodes perform unchecked arithmetic (gasLimit - gasUsed producing a negative long) in gas throttle restoration logic, cause unexpected behavior that destabilizes 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: gasConsumed] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction where gasConsumed is set to a value that overflows when added to intrinsic gas in SidecarProcessingResult.calculateGasConsumed (totalGasUsed + intrinsicGas), causing a silent long overflow that corrupts gas accounting and destabilizes throttle state on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: transactionHash] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract transaction with a transactionHash that is not 32 bytes (e.g., 0 bytes or 1000 bytes), and if toContractTransactionHash() passes this directly to ContractTransactionHash without length validation, cause a database constraint violation that crashes the importer batch on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: transactionNonce] Can an unprivileged user submit many contract transactions with the same transactionNonce value, and if importer nodes perform nonce-based deduplication or ordering that assumes uniqueness, cause processing deadlocks or infinite retry loops that hang 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: transactionIndex] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract transaction with a null transactionIndex, and if downstream code performs unboxing of the Integer transactionIndex field without null checks, cause a NullPointerException that crashes the processing thread on 30%+ of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: transactionResult] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract transaction with a null transactionResult, and because toContractTransactionHash() passes transactionResult directly to ContractTransactionHash.builder().transactionResult(transactionResult) without null checks, cause a NullPointerException that crashes the importer pipeline on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: contractId] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract transaction with contractId set to 0 or a negative value, and if downstream entity resolution code does not handle this edge case, cause an InvalidEntityException or similar error that is not recoverable, crashing the processing pipeline on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: senderId] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction where senderId resolves to an EntityId that is EntityId.EMPTY, and if toContractTransactionHash() or downstream code calls senderId.getId() on EMPTY without null-safety, cause a crash in the processing pipeline on 30%+ of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: payerAccountId] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract transaction where payerAccountId resolves to EntityId.EMPTY, and because toContractTransactionHash() calls payerAccountId.getId() unconditionally, cause a NullPointerException or zero-ID insertion that violates database constraints and crashes the importer on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: setBloom(byte[])] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract transaction with a bloom array of length != 256 (e.g., 1 byte or 10000 bytes), and because setBloom only checks Arrays.equals against EMPTY_BLOOM (which requires same length), cause the raw oversized bloom to be stored and later trigger a database column size violation that crashes the batch insert on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: bloom] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract transaction with a bloom field of zero length (empty byte array), and if downstream bloom filter logic in LogsBloomFilter.couldContain() treats a null/empty bloom as a positive match, cause incorrect event filtering that corrupts state on 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Field: functionParameters] Can an unprivileged user submit a contract call with functionParameters containing deeply nested ABI-encoded data structures, and if the importer's gas calculation in SidecarProcessingResult.calculateGasConsumed iterates over every byte of functionParameters, cause O(n) CPU consumption per transaction that degrades 30%+ of processing nodes under load?
